alignments.preprint_claim_text: IGRM allows for continuous friend network optimization during imputation learning.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about continuous optimization of friend networks is not explicitly restated in the publication's claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiment results show that IGRM yields 39.13% lower mean absolute error compared with nine baselines.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IGRM yields 39.13% lower mean absolute error compared with nine baselines.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IGRM achieves 9.04% lower mean absolute error than the second-best method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: IGRM achieves 9.04% lower mean absolute error than the second-best method.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed MUS-CDB method can save nearly 75% of the labeling cost while achieving comparable performance to other active learning methods in terms of mAP.

alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the key quantitative claim about label-cost savings; the preprint combined savings and performance into one sentence while the publication split them.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel active learning method incorporates both object-level and image-level informativeness to avoid redundant and myopic querying.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed active learning method incorporates both object-level and image-level informativeness to improve object selection.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same dual-level informativeness idea but frames it as improving object selection, a clarification of the intended effect.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An easy-to-use class-balancing criterion is included in the method to favor minority objects and address the long-tailed class distribution problem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An easy-to-use class-balancing criterion is included to address the long-tailed class distribution problem in model training.
alignments.rationale: The class-balancing contribution is stated equivalently in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing active learning methods are less applicable to aerial object detection scenarios due to the long-tailed class distribution and dense small objects.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate this limitation about existing methods' applicability, so the preprint's limitation statement is omitted as a standalone claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Anti-Blur Network (ABN) improves the accuracy of multi-stage image registration.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Anti-Blur Network (ABN) improves the accuracy of multi-stage image registration.
alignments.rationale: The main result about improved registration accuracy is restated identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ABN preserves image sharpness during the registration process.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ABN preserves image sharpness during the registration process.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the claim about preservation of image sharpness unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Conventional multi-stage registration methods can blur the source image due to repeated interpolation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of short-term registration and long-term memory networks in ABN allows for improved registration accuracy and preservation of image quality.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the proposed architectural combination as the reason for improved accuracy and image-quality preservation, clarifying the problem-and-solution relation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed solution uses a combination of short-term registration and long-term memory networks to learn nonlinear deformations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The combination of short-term registration and long-term memory networks in ABN allows for improved registration accuracy and preservation of image quality.
alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the benefit of the combined short-term/long-term design; this clarifies the contribution by linking it to outcomes.

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same claim verbatim about inferring geometry from dispersion, so meaning, scope, and confidence are unchanged.

alignments.rationale: The preprint asserts applicability of specific platforms, while the publication reframes this as an investigation of diffusion models generally, reducing assertiveness and broadening the class referenced.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A data set analysis of 40 million Midjourney queries reveals common usage patterns in architectural design. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: An analysis of 85 million Midjourney queries reveals common usage patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The core result (analysis of Midjourney queries revealing usage patterns) is the same, but the reported dataset size changed from 40M to 85M, a substantive revision.

alignments.rationale: Both describe derived workflows for interior/exterior design; the publication frames it more directly as derived from analysis and expresses higher certainty, clarifying and strengthening the claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework enables a visual representation of the complex structure of risk as a two-dimensional surface. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No change in wording or strength of claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a fundamental difference between explainable AI (XAI) and the Act regarding the concept of transparency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a fundamental difference between explainable AI (XAI) and the Act regarding the concept of transparency.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about a fundamental difference between XAI and the Act is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: XAI risks leaving a range of transparency issues unaddressed if the transparency gap is not addressed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: XAI risks leaving a range of transparency issues unaddressed if the transparency gap is not addressed.
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding unresolved transparency issues in XAI is carried over unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper identifies four axes where practical work could bridge the transparency gap between XAI and European regulation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper identifies four axes where practical work could bridge the transparency gap between XAI and European regulation.
alignments.rationale: The contribution listing four axes to bridge the gap is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed approach relies on a 3D multi-decoder residual convolutional neural network (CNN) with a U-Net architecture for multi-class segmentation of the mitral valve annulus and leaflets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed approach relies on a 3D multi-decoder residual convolutional neural network (CNN) with a U-Net architecture for multi-class segmentation of the mitral valve annulus and leaflets.
alignments.rationale: The architectural contribution is stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The trained 3D CNN achieves an average Dice score of $0.82  \\  0.06$, mean surface distance of $0.43  \\  0.14$ mm and 95% Hausdorff Distance (HD) of $3.57  \\  1.56$ mm before segmentation refinement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The trained 3D CNN achieves an average Dice score of 0.82 ± 0.06, mean surface distance of 0.43 ± 0.14 mm and 95% Hausdorff Distance (HD) of 3.57 ± 1.56 mm before segmentation refinement.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance metrics are the same; the publication only normalizes notation (±).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The automatic 3D linear morphological measurements of the annulus and leaflets exhibit differences of less than 1.45 mm when compared to ground truth values.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The automatic 3D linear morphological measurements of the annulus and leaflets exhibit differences of less than 1.45 mm when compared to ground truth values.
alignments.rationale: The measurement-difference result is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The whole process requires minimal user interaction and requires approximately 15 seconds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The whole process requires minimal user interaction and requires approximately 15 seconds.
alignments.rationale: The claim about minimal interaction and ~15s runtime is retained; the publication marks it as a result with higher certainty, so confidence is amplified.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The measurements from the CNN demonstrate strong overall agreement with analyses conducted by semi-automated commercial software.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears in the publication but was not present in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed FedST framework is a novel federated time series classification model that extends a centralized shapelet transformation method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed FedST framework is a novel federated time series classification model that extends a centralized shapelet transformation method.
alignments.rationale: The contribution describing FedST is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The basic protocol for the FedST kernel is proven to be secure and accurate.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The basic protocol for the FedST kernel is proven to be secure and accurate.
alignments.rationale: The security and accuracy proof for the basic protocol is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed optimizations for the federated protocol provide a security guarantee while improving efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed optimizations for the federated protocol provide a security guarantee while improving efficiency.
alignments.rationale: The optimizations' security and efficiency properties are stated the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical results demonstrate that the FedST solution is effective in terms of time series classification accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical results demonstrate that the FedST solution is effective in terms of time series classification accuracy.
alignments.rationale: Empirical effectiveness for classification accuracy is consistently reported.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed optimizations can achieve three orders of magnitude of speedup.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed optimizations can achieve three orders of magnitude of speedup.
alignments.rationale: The reported magnitude of speedup is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LIT-Former efficiently synergizes in-plane and through-plane sub-tasks for 3D CT imaging.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LIT-Former efficiently synergizes in-plane and through-plane sub-tasks for 3D CT imaging.
alignments.rationale: The central contribution about synergizing sub-tasks is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method significantly reduces computational complexity compared to 3D counterparts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method significantly reduces computational complexity compared to 3D counterparts.
alignments.rationale: The reduction in computational complexity is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate superior performance of LIT-Former over state-of-the-art models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate superior performance of LIT-Former over state-of-the-art models.
alignments.rationale: The performance advantage over SOTA is preserved without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LIT-Former incorporates efficient multi-head self-attention modules and efficient convolutional feedforward networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: LIT-Former incorporates efficient multi-head self-attention modules and efficient convolutional feed-forward networks.
alignments.rationale: The architectural details about attention and feedforward networks are present in both versions with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Spacecraft U-shaped Network (SU-Net) achieves end-to-end pose estimation for non-cooperative spacecraft.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep learning neural network architecture, named SU-Net, effectively extracts orbiting spacecraft features from ISAR images for pose estimation.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the contribution to emphasize feature extraction from ISAR images for pose estimation rather than the broader "end-to-end" claim for non-cooperative spacecraft, narrowing the scope and clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Dense Residual U-shaped Network (DR-U-Net) reduces feature loss and model complexity while avoiding degradation during training.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about DR-U-Net reducing feature loss and complexity and avoiding degradation does not appear in the publication and is not reflected by any published claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates robust performance and outperforms state-of-the-art pose estimation methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that SU-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance in pose estimation, with an absolute error ranging from 0.128° to 0.4491° and a mean error of about 0.282°.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the performance claim but provides specific numeric error ranges and mean, clarifying the reported results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The absolute error of the pose estimation is between 0.1557 and 0.4491, with a mean error of about 0.302 and a standard deviation of about 0.065.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that SU-Net achieves state-of-the-art performance in pose estimation, with an absolute error ranging from 0.128° to 0.4491° and a mean error of about 0.282°.
alignments.rationale: The numeric error bounds and mean are reported slightly differently in the publication; this is a clarification/adjustment of the reported statistics rather than a substantive reversal.

alignments.pub_claim_text: SU-Net improves spacecraft imaging by enhancing image contrast, reducing noise, and utilizing transfer learning to address data sparsity issues.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds claims about imaging improvements and transfer learning that were not present in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a multi-head self-attention block in SU-Net captures more global information and improves the model's accuracy.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about adding a multi-head self-attention block and its effect on global information and accuracy appears in the publication but was not in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a lack of coherency on the grading provided by some of the considered evaluation methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a lack of coherency on the grading provided by some of the considered evaluation methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are essentially identical in wording and meaning, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Some characteristics of the explanations, such as sparsity, can have a significant effect on the performance of evaluation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Some characteristics of the explanations, such as sparsity, can have a significant effect on the performance of evaluation methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation methods relying on synthetic datasets have limited guarantees regarding their applicability in more realistic settings. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation methods for neural network explanations may not perform consistently with respect to each other. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the limitation from dataset-specific applicability to inconsistent performance between methods, changing the focus and lowering stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combined metadata features are the best predictors of wealth in rural areas, outperforming image-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combined metadata features are the best predictors of wealth in rural areas, outperforming image-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is repeated exactly with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models recover the local mean and variation of wealth and correctly capture the positive yet non-monotonous correlation between them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The models recover the local mean and variation of wealth and correctly capture the positive yet non-monotonous correlation between them. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical result claim in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology provides open tools to build towards more transparent and interpretable models for informed decision-making by governments and NGOs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methodology provides open tools to help governments and NGOs make informed decisions based on data availability, urbanization level, and poverty thresholds. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same contribution but adds concrete decision factors (data availability, urbanization, thresholds), clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The capabilities and limitations of model transfer across countries and the effects of data recency and other biases are demonstrated. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study demonstrates the capabilities and limitations of model transfer across countries and the effects of data recency and other biases. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates this evaluation result/conclusion with effectively the same content and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed models illustrate performance in Sierra Leone and Uganda. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This publication-level claim about specific country illustrations is not present in the preprint and appears as an added concrete example.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework for scribble-based salient object detection outperforms state-of-the-art weakly supervised methods without introducing any extra labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework for scribble-based salient object detection outperforms state-of-the-art weakly supervised methods without introducing any extra labels. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried forward verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves an average gain of 5.5% for max f-measure, 5.8% for mean f-measure, 24% for MAE, and 3.1% for E-measure compared to the previous best scribble-based method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results show an average gain of 5.5% for max f-measure, 5.8% for mean f-measure, 24% for MAE, and 3.1% for E-measure compared to the previous best scribble-based method. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Numeric experimental gains are reproduced exactly in the publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework utilizes general visual representations generated by self-supervised learning based on large-scale unlabeled datasets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework utilizes general visual representations generated by self-supervised learning based on large-scale unlabeled datasets. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is retained verbatim in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method achieves comparable or superior performance to state-of-the-art fully supervised models. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method achieves comparable or superior performance to state-of-the-art fully supervised models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is the same but the published version expresses higher certainty (amplified confidence).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed global semantic affinity loss guides the model to perceive the global structure of salient objects. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This specific contribution (global semantic affinity loss) is not present among the publication claims and appears to have been omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Physics-Informed Long Short-Term Memory (PI-LSTM) network correctly predicts the statistics of the unmeasured variables in a chaotic system when constrained by physical laws. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The PI-LSTM correctly predicts the statistics of the unmeasured variables when constrained by the physical laws, which cannot be achieved without this constraint. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the core result about correctly predicting statistics under physical constraints, adding the point that this is not achievable without the constraint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding the physics-informed loss qualitatively enhances the dynamical behavior of the PI-LSTM network compared to data-driven only training. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Adding the physics-informed loss qualitatively enhances the dynamical behavior of the network compared to data-driven only training. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The qualitative improvement from the physics-informed loss is preserved with similar wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The computation of Lyapunov exponents of the PI-LSTM network infers key stability properties of the chaotic system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Lyapunov exponents computed from the network indicate key stability properties of the chaotic system. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim content is the same but the published version reports a lower certainty level (high -> medium).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work with PI-LSTM opens up new opportunities for state reconstruction and learning of the dynamics of nonlinear systems. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work opens up new opportunities for state reconstruction and learning of the dynamics of nonlinear systems. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution is retained but the publication expresses lower certainty about the future opportunities (medium -> low).

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed data-driven prognostics approach improves the reliability and safety of proton exchange membrane fuel cells (PEMFC). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion is identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hilbert-Huang transform is effective in extracting health indicators under dynamic operating conditions for PEMFC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hilbert-Huang transform is effective in extracting health indicators for PEMFC operating under dynamic load conditions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The symbolic-based gated recurrent unit model enhances the accuracy of life prediction for PEMFC. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The symbolic-based gated recurrent unit model enhances the accuracy of life prediction for PEMFC. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach provides a competitive prognostics horizon with lower computational cost compared to other state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach provides a competitive prognostics horizon with lower computational cost compared to other state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The prognostics performance of the proposed approach shows consistency and generalizability under different failure threshold settings. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The prognostics performance of the proposed approach shows consistency and generalizability under different failure threshold settings. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method achieves 60% lower bitrate than a state-of-the-art SC method without decreasing accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method achieves 60% lower bitrate than a state-of-the-art Split Computing method without decreasing accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is up to 16x faster than offloading with existing codec standards. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method is up to 16x faster than offloading with existing codec standards. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work proposes shifting focus from executing shallow layers of partitioned DNNs to concentrating local resources on variational compression optimized for machine interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: This work proposes shifting focus from executing shallow layers of partitioned DNNs to concentrating local resources on variational compression optimized for machine interpretability. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Participants preferred the representation of data-centric explanations that provide local explanations with a global overview over other methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The explanation dashboard assists healthcare experts in monitoring the risk of diabetes onset and recommending measures to minimize risk. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study highlights the importance of visually directive data-centric explanation methods for assisting healthcare experts to gain actionable insights from patient health records. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The utility of different explanation methods in guiding healthcare experts for patient care remains under-researched. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The utility of different explanation methods in guiding healthcare experts for patient care remains under-researched. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation is unchanged between versions in content and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Deep neural networks exhibit a learnability advantage for more structured linguistic input. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural networks exposed to more compositional languages show more systematic generalization. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both deep neural networks and humans show greater agreement between different agents when learning more compositional languages. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Deep neural networks demonstrate greater similarity to human learners when exposed to compositional languages. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The behavior of deep neural networks is more similar to human learners when they are exposed to compositional languages. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the claim with clearer wording and higher stated certainty, preserving scope but amplifying confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpanKL significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art models in many aspects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpanKL significantly outperforms previous state-of-the-art models in many aspects. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result is unchanged in wording, scope, and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SpanKL obtains the smallest gap from continual learning to the upper bound, revealing its high practical value. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: SpanKL obtains the smallest gap from continual learning to the upper bound, revealing its high practical value. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is preserved verbatim with the same implications and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The independent modeling in span and entity level with coherent optimization in SpanKL promotes learning at each incremental step and mitigates forgetting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The independent modeling in span and entity level with coherent optimization in SpanKL promotes learning at each incremental step and mitigates forgetting. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end solution accurately detects fake news and immunizes network nodes that spread them in real-time. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Two new stack deep learning architectures utilizing convolutional and bidirectional LSTM layers are proposed for detecting fake news. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A real-time network-aware strategy is proposed to mitigate the spread of fake news by constructing a minimum-cost weighted directed spanning tree. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of the solution is demonstrated on five real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the solution is demonstrated on five real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper proposes a paradigm of uncertainty injection for training deep learning models to solve robust optimization problems. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed uncertainty injection scheme allows machine learning models to implicitly account for uncertainties and produce statistically robust solutions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed uncertainty injection scheme allows machine learning models to implicitly account for uncertainties and produce statistically robust solutions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The effectiveness of the proposed training scheme is demonstrated in robust power loading for multiuser MIMO downlink transmissions and robust power control for D2D networks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Early detection and intervention can reduce the risk of depression in COVID-19 patients. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep neural network model outperforms baseline methods in detecting depression risk, achieving an AUROC of 0.9317 and an AUPRC of 0.8116. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Language models of sufficient scale demonstrate in-context learning, allowing them to learn from associations between a small number of input patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Language models of sufficient scale demonstrate in-context learning, allowing them to learn from associations between a small number of input patterns. (certainty=high, type=result)
